warrens-thinning.name: Thin the Warrens
warrens-thinning.description: Maeve needs the warren rats culled — and their meat for the inn's pot.

start.offerText: "The rats are breeding fast in the warren below. Cull the brood and bring me the meat — the pot's been thin all week, and I'll pay you in hot food and shards."

steps.text: Cull 5 giant rats in the warren
Bring 5 cuts of rat meat to Maeve

vespers-caps.name: Caps for the Mage
vespers-caps.description: Vesper needs glowing witchglow caps from the deep clusters for her glimmer-work.

start.offerText: Vesper looks up from her bench. "Headed down into the dark? Then do me a turn — witchglow caps grow on the clusters below, dripping that cold green light. Pick me a few and bring them back; I render their glow into my work, and I pay for the trouble."

steps.text: Pick 3 witchglow caps from a witchglow cluster
Bring the witchglow caps to Vesper

glimmer-for-vesper.name: A Shard of Light
glimmer-for-vesper.description: A whole glimmer crystal is a rare find — Vesper would give much to study one intact.

start.offerText: The crystal pulses with trapped light, whole and uncracked — a rare thing to pull from the dark unbroken. Vesper the glimmer-mage would give a great deal to study one like this.

steps.text: Bring the glimmer crystal to Vesper in the Mage's Shed

wick-stalker.name: Something in the Pens
wick-stalker.description: Something is killing Wick's stonebugs and dragging them off — and it must be denning somewhere close to the stockpens.

start.offerText: "I'm losing stonebugs," Wick says, low and angry. "Not to the pot — to whatever's been getting into the pens at night. Three this week, dragged clean off, nothing left but cracked shell. It's denning somewhere close, I'd swear it. Go and search the ground around the pens — the dead yard out east, the back corners, anywhere a thing could squeeze in. Find it, kill it, and I'll teach you to brew your own light for the trouble."

steps.text: Search the ground around the stockpens and kill whatever has been taking Wick's stonebugs

wick-night-wing.name: Something Worse in the Pens
wick-night-wing.description: A monstrous bat out of the spire has taken to raiding Wick's pens at night — bigger and bolder than anything that denned there before.

start.offerText: "Thought I'd seen the worst of it with that centipede," Wick says, jaw tight. "Now something's coming down out of the spire at night — big, black, wings like a torn cloak. Tore a pen clean open and carried off the lot, stock and all. That's no denning thing, that's flying down from the roost up there. I want it dead before it takes something bigger than a stonebug."

steps.text: Kill the huge bat raiding Wick's pens from the roost in the Bat Spire

fenn-quiet-claim.name: Quiet Too Long
fenn-quiet-claim.description: Marl Wender signed down to the fourth level three seasons ago and never signed back up. Fenn's register wants certainty before he closes the name.

start.offerText: Fenn opens the chained ledger to a page near the back and taps a name underlined twice. "Wender, M. Signed down to the fourth level, three seasons gone, and never signed back up. The register wants certainty before I close a name. Every delver I register wears a tag below — find his, wherever he ended, and lay it on this counter. Start where the water ends up. The lake keeps most of what the deep takes."

steps.text: Find Marl Wender's tag, wherever the deep left him
Lay the tag on Fenn's counter

board-crawler-bounty.name: Bounty: Crawlers on the Descent
board-crawler-bounty.description: The traders pay a standing bounty on the pale crawlers that hunt the dim margins below the descent.

start.offerText: A posting in Fenn's careful copy-hand: "BOUNTY, STANDING. The pale crawlers work the dim margins off the First Dark and take the new-come and the wounded — and the lurkers drop on them from the dark overhead. Seven crawlers and three lurkers culled keeps the descent passable a while. Payment on the traders' purse."

steps.text: Cull 7 gloom-crawlers below the descent
Cull 3 cave lurkers below the descent

board-tallow-order.name: Standing Order: Tallow
board-tallow-order.description: Garrick renders bug-tallow into lamp-oil and pays a flat rate for lumps of it, no questions asked.

start.offerText: A posting gone translucent with grease at one corner: "STANDING ORDER. Bug-tallow, by the lump, flat rate — the lamps don't fill themselves. Bring it to Garrick at the market stall. Don't haggle. He won't."

steps.text: Bring 10 lumps of bug-tallow to Garrick

heart-of-the-dark.name: The Heart of the Dark
heart-of-the-dark.description: A shadow-heart, cut cold from the Starving Dark — deep-folk's work, not the surface's. Mallki, the Umbral trader by the river, is the one who would understand it.

start.offerText: The shadow-heart sits cold and restless in your pack, drinking the light off your fingers. This is no surface trophy — it is deep-folk's matter, and Mallki, the qhatuq by the river, is the one who would know the binding of it.

steps.text: Carry the shadow-heart to Mallki by the river

smiths-hammer.name: The Tinker's Hammer
smiths-hammer.description: A rusted hammer from the deep, struck with the Rim smith's own maker's mark. Tobin would want this back.

start.offerText: Under the rust, a maker's mark — and you have seen it before, stamped into half the gear on the Craftsmen's Row. This is Tobin's mark. The tinker-smith struck this hammer, and someone carried it down into the dark and never brought it home. He would want it back.

steps.text: Carry the rusted hammer to Tobin on the Craftsmen's Row

kings-shell.name: The King's Shell
kings-shell.description: The Pale King's carapace is armour enough for a delver, if it can be worked — and the surface has no smith who would know where to begin. Mallki, the Umbral trader, works the deep's own shell, and would want to see a kingshell plate.

start.offerText: The kingshell plate is heavy as a shield and harder, apex shell crusted with raw glimmer — armour, if a hand that knew the deep's craft could work it. Mallki, the qhatuq by the river, is that hand.

steps.text: Bring the kingshell plate to Mallki by the river

mallki-lost-blade.name: The Blade He Lost
mallki-lost-blade.description: Mallki lost an old deep-stone blade on a climb toward the surface. He is sentimental about it, and would have it back.

start.offerText: Mallki turns a glimmer-lamp low and is quiet a moment. "Once I carried a blade up toward your sun, yaku-runa — deep-stone, glimmer-veined, shaped by my own hand. The dark took it from me on the climb, somewhere near the rim, and I have not gone up to look. I am... fond of it." His lightless eyes settle on you. "Bring it back to me, and I will shape you one of your own. A better one."

steps.text: Find Mallki's lost blade, somewhere up near the rim
Return the blade to Mallki by the river

fenn-prospectors-bane.name: The Prospectors' Bane
fenn-prospectors-bane.description: Registered prospectors working the seams east of the warren have stopped coming up — an outlaw crew that seized a working below is robbing and killing honest diggers. Fenn wants the crew broken and their leader put down so the ground is safe to work again.

start.offerText: Fenn turns the ledger toward you and taps a column of names, each with a date beside it and nothing written after. "Prospectors. Registered, paid their fees, went down to work the seams east of the warren — and stopped coming up. This is no quiet-too-long; I know the dark's hand when I see it, and this isn't it. There's a crew seized a working down there, and they've taken to robbing and killing honest diggers for the little they carry. I can't send Hale below the gate, and no prospector will go back while that lot hold the ground. Break them — thin the crew and put down the one they call the Foreman — so a man can swing a pick down there and live to spend what he digs. Do that, and the office will see you well paid."

steps.text: Thin the outlaw crew preying on honest prospectors east of the warren
Put down the Foreman, the crew's leader

fenn-lost-hermit.name: The Name That Never Signed Up
fenn-lost-hermit.description: An early name in Fenn's register — an old prospector who came down a few years back, before the main Rush, and never signed back up. Fenn heard plenty of him in his day; too long gone now to be a quiet-claim, but the name nags him — and word says a lamplit garden still keeps far down in the Gloaming.

start.offerText: Fenn thumbs back through the register to an early page and lays a finger on a name signed down and never signed up again. "Halloran. Came down a few years back — early, before the big Rush, before the crowds — an old hand even then, and I heard plenty of him in those days: went below to dig and never came up. By rights I'd have marked him lost seasons ago, same as the rest." He does not. "But a prospector through here last season spoke of a lamp still burning far down in the Gloaming, and a garden, and an old man who talks to his stonebugs. I'll not strike a living man off my own book on hearsay. You're headed down that way — find him, past the fruiting hollows they say, and bring me some sign he's still drawing breath."

steps.text: Find Old Halloran in the deep Gloaming (past the Fruiting Bower) and get from him some sign he still lives
Carry Halloran's tag up to Fenn's counter

halloran-brood-source.name: The Source of the Rot
halloran-brood-source.description: The nest at the throat spills its vermin down into Halloran's green faster than he can cut them back. He wants the source — the Brood-Mother in the deepest hollow of the nest — put down, and a fang brought back as proof the runs will go quiet.

start.offerText: Halloran straightens from a bed, wiping soil on his knees, and looks at you a long moment before he speaks. "You came down past the choke — you'll have seen the runs. The gnaw-marks, the droppings, that little grey tide that never quite stops. That's not my garden's own trouble; that's the nest, up at the throat, spilling its get down into my green faster than the blooms can eat them. I've cut and I've cut, but you don't save a bed by pulling weeds — you go for the root. Something old and swollen mothers the whole reeking lot from the deepest hollow of that nest, and every litter she drops climbs up past me. Thin her grown get on your way in — the warped ones, bold enough they don't scatter — then put down the bloated thing at the heart of it. And bring me a fang off her jaw, so I know it's truly done. I'll not keep the thing; it's yours, and worth more to a climber than to an old man rooted here. I only need to see it."

steps.text: Cull the nest's warped get on the way into the deep warren
Put down the Brood-Mother in the deepest hollow of the nest
Carry a warp-fang back to Halloran as proof the source is dead

proving-culling.name: The Trial of the Brood
proving-culling.description: The paqo's blessing rings the Culling Pit's rim — the village has warded the bloated thing denned below shut rather than dig it free, and now asks a prospector to put it down.

start.offerText: A marker of chalked glyphs rings the pit's mouth, cut by the paqo's own hand and carrying her blessing down into the dark: "Whatever has denned here, the ward has held it and no more. Go down, prospector, and put it under — not for glory, but so the ward may rest. Bring back what it leaves, and the shrine will know the trial kept."

steps.text: Put down the Bloated Horror denned in the Culling Pit
Bring the severed feeler to the paqo at the shrine

proving-shadewell.name: The Trial of the Dark
proving-shadewell.description: The paqo's blessing is cut into the Shadewell's rim by feel, not sight — something has drunk that pit's light dry for longer than the village can say, and the trial asks a prospector to end it.

start.offerText: Where the last light gives out, the paqo's blessing is cut into the rim by feel alone, meant to be read by touch as much as by eye: "No lamp holds long in the Shadewell, and no lamp should. Go down blind if you must, and put an end to what drinks the dark there. Bring back what it has swallowed, that the shrine may know the debt paid."

steps.text: Put an end to the Kept Dark denned in the Shadewell
Bring the knot of un-drunk light to the paqo at the shrine

proving-seam.name: The Trial of the Amaru
proving-seam.description: The paqo's blessing is cut deepest of the three at the Deep Seam's mouth — the oldest denning the village still keeps warded, and the last trial seals a prospector in with it until one of the two is dead.

start.offerText: You drop into the seam and the light dies, and only then do you read the paqo's blessing cut into the shaft-wall by the sealed grate above: "There is no climbing back but by the ward, and the ward lifts only for one who carries proof the thing below is slain. Go down certain, prospector, or do not go down. The Amaru or you — the seam keeps whichever it can."

steps.text: Put down the Amaru denned in the Deep Seam
Bring a fang of the Amaru to the paqo at the shrine
